label: Additionality adjustment default rates (Australia)
note: Australia has no single equivalent to the UK HMT Additionality Guide. These defaults are calibrated from Infrastructure Australia guidance, state treasury practices, and Australian evaluation literature. Adjust based on context.

standard.label: Standard (central estimates for Australian context)
standard.note: Central estimates. Leakage is slightly higher than UK defaults due to more open regional economies (larger geographic distances, fewer contiguous labour markets).

formula: net_additional = gross * (1 - deadweight) * (1 - displacement) * (1 - leakage) * (1 - substitution) * multiplier

sector_defaults.description: Sector-specific defaults estimated from Australian evaluation evidence and Infrastructure Australia practice.

mining.note: Low deadweight (projects typically would not proceed without intervention), but high leakage (FIFO workforce, imported equipment).

retail_hospitality.note: High displacement: new retail/hospitality venues often take trade from existing businesses.

leakage.note: Australian regions are more geographically isolated than UK LAs, but FIFO/DIDO workforce patterns increase leakage in resource regions.

source.publication: Derived from Infrastructure Australia Assessment Framework, state treasury guidance (VIC DTF, NSW TPG23-08), and Australian evaluation literature
